parts.description: Claude Code skill encoding the full conda-forge recipe lifecycle. 3-tier architecture: canonical scripts + CLI wrappers + data state. 10-step autonomous loop with one human-gated checkpoint at step 8b (prepare_submission_branch). Authoritative spine: SKILL.md.

subdirectories.SKILL.md: primary spine: critical constraints, 10-step autonomous loop, recipe authoring gotchas G1-G6
subdirectories.CHANGELOG.md: release history with TL;DR section (canonical drift-detection source)
subdirectories.reference/: 13 deep-reference files (recipe-yaml-reference, meta-yaml-reference, mcp-tools, python-min-policy, conda-forge-yml-reference, pinning-reference, selectors-reference, jinja-functions, dependency-input-formats, atlas-actionable-intelligence, atlas-phases-overview, atlas-phase-engineering, conda-forge-ecosystem)
subdirectories.guides/: 8 workflow guides (getting-started, migration, ci-troubleshooting, cross-compilation, feedstock-maintenance, testing-recipes, sdist-missing-license, atlas-operations)
subdirectories.scripts/: ~30 canonical Python implementations (single source of truth for behavior)
subdirectories.tests/: pytest suite (unit + integration + meta) — uses real fixtures, no network mocking

parts.description: Offline-tolerant package-intelligence pipeline that builds and maintains cf_atlas.db (SQLite, schema v22) inventorying conda-forge packages (32k working set) plus a separate pypi_universe side table (the full PyPI directory, ~800k rows) plus a pypi_intelligence side table (35-column enrichment layer indexed on pypi_name, 5 tiers). 22 ordered phases (B→N + O/P/Q/R/S, with B.5/B.6/C.5/E.5/G' sub-phases) with TTL gates and checkpoint-aware resumption. Air-gap-tolerant via S3-parquet (Phase F) and cf-graph offline (Phase H) backends. v7.9.0 actionable-scope audit closed phase-denominator drift across Phase H/J/M and split Phase D into daily-lean + TTL-gated universe upsert. v8.0.0 structural-enforcement + persona-profiles bundle ships schema v21's v_actionable_packages view (7 phase selectors refactored) + Phase H serial-aware eligible-rows gate (warm-daily ~5 min → ~30 s) + tests/meta/test_actionable_scope.py + bootstrap-data --profile {maintainer,admin,consumer} with gh-user + phase-L-sources auto-detection. v8.1.0 PyPI intelligence layer adds 5 new phases (O snapshot-based activity_band; P BigQuery downloads opt-in; Q cross-channel BOOLs; R per-project JSON enrichment for top-N candidate slice; S computed conda_forge_readiness + recommended_template), pypi-intelligence CLI + MCP tool, and the pypi_intelligence side table with v_pypi_candidates view.

data_artifacts.cf-graph-countyfair.tar.gz: cf-graph offline snapshot (used by Phase E/H/M)
data_artifacts.cache/parquet/: Phase F S3 monthly parquet cache (created on demand, ~13MB/month)

parts.description: FastMCP server exposing 36 MCP tools across recipe-authoring, atlas-intelligence, and project-scanning surfaces. Auto-started by Claude Code at session boot. Each tool is a thin wrapper around a Tier-1 canonical script from Part 1.

tool_surfaces.recipe-authoring: ~15 tools: generate_recipe_from_pypi, validate_recipe, edit_recipe, optimize_recipe, scan_for_vulnerabilities, trigger_build, get_build_summary, analyze_build_failure, prepare_submission_branch, submit_pr, migrate_to_v1, update_recipe_from_github, update_recipe, update_cve_database, update_mapping_cache
tool_surfaces.atlas-intelligence: ~13 tools: query_atlas, package_health, staleness_report, cve_watcher, behind_upstream, feedstock_health, whodepends, release_cadence, version_downloads, find_alternative, adoption_stage, pypi_only_candidates, my_feedstocks, lookup_feedstock, get_feedstock_context, enrich_from_feedstock, check_dependencies, check_github_version, get_conda_name, get_build_summary, analyze_build_failure
tool_surfaces.project-scanning: ~5 tools: scan_project (~28 input formats), run_system_health_check, trigger_build, prepare_submission_branch, submit_pr

parts.description: BMAD-METHOD v6.6.0 installer + multi-project planning layout. Six-layer config merge, 65 installed skills, active-project resolution via bmad-switch. Hosts multiple BMAD projects (local-recipes + sibling projects) in a single repo.
parts.installed_skills_note: Current repo state is 65 entries in .claude/skills/ but one (.claude/skills/data/) is a stray directory with no SKILL.md inside (contains only an old conda-forge-expert/ subdir). Real skill count is 64: 42 BMAD-installer + 21 engineering-practice + 1 repo-specific (conda-forge-expert). Rebuild target = 64 real skills; the stray is not recreated.

integration_points.details: Both parts read/write .claude/data/conda-forge-expert/ — cf_atlas.db is Part 2's primary artifact; Part 1's edit_recipe / scan_for_vulnerabilities / generate_recipe_from_pypi consume it via query helpers.

Each MCP tool in conda_forge_server.py imports from .claude/skills/conda-forge-expert/scripts/<module>.py and forwards args. The MCP server is the wire format; the canonical implementation is Part 1's scripts.
Atlas-intelligence tools (query_atlas, package_health, staleness_report, etc.) read cf_atlas.db directly via the canonical scripts in Part 1.

Every BMAD agent (planning, dev, review, retro) touching conda-forge work MUST invoke the conda-forge-expert skill via Skill tool. Codified in CLAUDE.md § 'BMAD ↔ conda-forge-expert integration'. The skill's 9-step loop + critical constraints override BMAD story instructions when they conflict.

Every BMAD effort that did conda-forge work runs bmad-retrospective at closeout. Retro findings update SKILL.md, reference/*.md, guides/*.md, and CHANGELOG.md. Skill version bumps per semver (PATCH for fixes, MINOR for new gotchas, MAJOR for breaking workflow changes). Codified in CLAUDE.md § 'BMAD ↔ conda-forge-expert integration'.

Every outbound HTTP request from Parts 1, 2, 3 routes through .claude/skills/conda-forge-expert/scripts/_http.py — truststore (native CA) + JFrog X-JFrog-Art-Api header (when JFROG_API_KEY set) + GitHub token + .netrc fallback. CRITICAL: JFROG_API_KEY leaks to every host when set; mitigation patterns in docs/enterprise-deployment.md § 2 Cross-host credential leak.

vuln-db pixi env provides AppThreat vulnerability database (NVD + GHSA + OSV + npm + Snyk). Part 1's scan_for_vulnerabilities and Part 2's Phase G/G' read from .claude/data/conda-forge-expert/vdb/. Separate env keeps the default local-recipes env lean (AppThreat pulls ~500MB).

rebuild_dependencies.reasoning: BMAD infra must exist for any planning to happen. The skill (Part 1) is foundational — Parts 2 and 3 are essentially extensions of it (Part 2 = the data pipeline encoded in the skill's scripts/; Part 3 = the MCP wire format over the skill's scripts/). Build order matches dependency order.
